title: MIN_TRENDLINE_BEAR_SPREAD_CENTS=35 for SAFE TRENDLINE-only BEAR entries
verdict_reason: G1 FAIL (IS_delta=-11). Root cause: analysis had a timezone bug that invalidated the A/B test.

timezone_bug.description: entry_time_et stores naive ET strings (option CSV convention). A/B test applied tz_localize('UTC') instead of tz_localize('America/New_York'), causing a ~5h offset. The ribbon spread values were actually pulled from premarket bars (not RTH entry-time bars). With correct timezone handling, the IS spread values changed completely.
timezone_bug.lesson: ALL future A/B tests using entry_time_et must use tz_localize('America/New_York') — not UTC.

IS_removed.note: includes +$92 winner (2025-02-24, spread=19c) and +$35 winner (2026-02-04, spread=28c)

no_valid_threshold: No threshold in 1c-100c passes G1 AND G2 simultaneously. To block OOS loser at 34c requires threshold >=35c. At >=35c, IS 2025-02-24 (19c spread, +$92) is also blocked. IS_delta becomes negative at all thresholds that capture the OOS improvement.
implementation_status: Reverted. Orchestrator: gate kept as dormant knob (default 0.0 = disabled, no production effect). params.json: NOT updated.
follow_up: TRENDLINE-only bear ribbon spread is NOT a useful entry-time gate. Hypothesis disproven. Next: analyze OOS bull losses (-$173 OOS, n=8) — a more impactful research direction.
